Creating Prelaunch Content, Part 3
!
Here's part three of Creating Prelaunch Content.
!
We're going to talk about creating a screen cap video.
!
There are many, many types of screen cap. You can create it as slides like I'm doing
right now. You create a presentation in PowerPoint or in Keynote.
!
You can do a mind map or a process map.
!
I have done this many times, in fact I am pretty sure that I pioneered the whole idea of
doing a screen capture video with a mind map.
!
The first time I rolled out Project Launch Formula, that was one of the things that I did
both in the product and the pre-launch. That blew people’s mind back then. That was
eight years ago. No one did it before I did it, at least that I ever saw, at least in this
market. I really pioneered that. Lots and lots of people have gone on to do that.
!
You can also screen cap a blog or a social media site.
!
This is great for social proof later in your prelaunch. Maybe your third piece of pre-
launch content or maybe even do a piece of content after your cart is open where you
go, if you have a blog post and there’s a lot of great comments on that blog post or if
you have a social media site where people are leaving you great comments and
testimonials.
!
Fire up your screen cap and go to that site and then go through some of the comments.
Go through some of the discussion. Go through your blog. Go through some of the
discussions, some of the comments and it’s a really, really powerful way to show if
people are saying great things about you or if you’ve got a mention in social media.
Shoot a video of that. It can be very, very powerful social proof if you’re actually
showing people what other people think about you around the web.
!
Along the same lines, you could do it in email. If someone sent you a great testimonial
via email and just like showing some text on a screen, you can actually fire up your
screen capture, navigate to your email program and open up the email. It just looks so
much more real and so much more authentic.
!
This is something I did way back in 2005 and it was super effective back then. It’s still
effective. It’s big time social proof when you do something like that. You can do it for a
membership site, it could be your third piece of prelaunch content. If you’re selling a
membership site, you can take them for a virtual tour where you’re opening up the
membership site and then you’re navigating around and showing what’s going on and
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2

-- 2 of 5 --

you’re capturing that entire experience. You’re giving people an experience of going
inside your product. It can be very, very powerful.
!
Another one is software. If you are selling software, give a tour of the software. You
can show how the software works, you can show the results you get from the software-
very, very effective way to use screen capture.
!
In terms of screen cap, how do you actually do this? What are the tools we use?
!
There are lots and lots of tools and they keep on getting better and better and better.
I’ve been using screen capture for over a decade I think, since 2003. The tools just get
better all along.
!
On the Mac, I use ScreenFlow. That’s the name of the program and it’s not a terribly
expensive program. I think it might be 100 bucks. That’s actually what I’m using right
now to record this is ScreenFlow. They keep on coming out with new versions. They
have really great upgrade policies and it has gotten so much better than it was when it
started out.
You can also use Camtasia.
!
Camtasia is a really powerful program on Windows. I’m not as a big a fan of it on Mac
the last time I looked. Of course they’re constantly coming out with new updates as
well. ScreenFlow and Camtasia are very competitive with each other. I don’t know, I
have not looked recently maybe Camtasia got a lot better. The last time I looked, I
actually bought both of them and I tested them out. I liked ScreenFlow better but again
it could be different by the time you are watching this.
!
Then there’s also Quicktime. That is built right into your Mac. You basically have a free
screen capture tool right within Quicktime where you can just make a screen recording
is what they call it. Not as powerful or as flexible as ScreenFlow or Camtasia but the
price is right. It’s a great place to test it out. On the Mac here, there are a bunch of
other screen capture tools as well but ScreenFlow and Camtasia are certainly the top of
the heap the last time I looked.
!
Now, Windows it’s a lot more limited. That’s the bad news. The good news is that
Camtasia is a great program in Windows. I haven’t used this in several years because I
switched over to Mac several years ago but when I used Windows I used Camtasia
because it’s just an extraordinary program. It is quite a bit more expensive over on the
Windows the last time I looked. It was like $300 or something like that so it’s very
expensive but there is a 30-day free trial. You could check out the 30-day free trial. It’s
completely unlimited so you have 30 days. You could literally get the trial, learn how to
use it, create an entire prelaunch and create your product and release it and pay for it
theoretically before you even need to actually buy it. It’s just a great free trial. It’s a
great program.

-- 3 of 5 --

!
There’s also a free one called CamStudio. This one, it’s been bought and sold a few
times. At one point it was free then it wasn’t free. I just went and looked. It looks like
right now it’s free but this thing has bounced around quite a bit. I have never used it.
I’ve heard of other people using it. How it compares to Camtasia? I don’t know but I
just put it on there because it might be something you might want to check out.
!
Now, continuing on the tools front, that software will capture the screen for you and it
will capture audio for you but you have to worry about quality. Audio quality is very
important with your video. This goes for whether you are using screen capture or full
motion video.
!
The audio quality is very important. Use an external mic, don’t use your computer’s
built-in mic as a general rule. The built-in mics in the computer keep on getting better
but I will still use an external mic because they’re not expensive. Headset mics are
cheap and they work great. That’s what I’m using right now. The great thing about
them is they aren’t impacted by the recording environment.
For an example... one time I created a screen cap video at my home. Then I was in my
office and I decided I wanted to add some to it. I had my headset mic with me. Even
though it was a completely different recording environment I was able to just fire up my
screen cap, record some audio and then plug that right in and you couldn’t tell the
difference.
!
If you use a desktop mic or anything else other than a headset mic, it’s very susceptible
to your recording environment - the room you’re in, the placement of the mic is critical,
what the walls are made up, what kind of floor covering you have - can all impact a
regular mic that’s either on your desktop or on a stand. It’s very susceptible to the
environment. If you change your environment then you can’t just go and mix different
audios from different environments.
!
That one I told you I recorded with a headset and I recorded part in my office, part at
my home and then I actually had to take a trip and I flew across the country actually to
a different country and I was in my hotel room. I realized that I needed to make some
changes. They weren’t big but just a few changes to that screen cap video. I brought
my headset microphone just in case so I’m sitting there in my hotel room in a different
country recording an audio and you couldn’t tell the difference between the different
audios just because of the nature of a headset mic with your closed mic, meaning the
mic is very close to your mouth, not so impacted by your external environment, by the
recording environment.
!
That’s why I like them and I tend to use them. Also, they tend be very cheap and they
work great. I’ve got a few of them. The one I’m using right now is by Plantronics. It
was probably about a 120-dollar mic. I’ve got one by Sennheiser I really like a lot. I just
got a new one from Logitech. It’s a little bit smaller. I wanted something smaller that
would fit in my bag when I travelled-any of those brands.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4
PLF_Module4v1.0

-- 4 of 5 --

!
You can get a decent headset for 30 bucks, a decent headset mic for 30 bucks. I think
the ones I’ve got I’ve spent mostly between 70 and 120, $150. Get a USB headset so it
just plugs right in to your computer. Don’t get one with those little jacks, those little
mini jacks with the XLR inputs. Just get one with USB, it makes it a lot simpler for you.
!
If you’re actually going to be on camera I would use a mic on your desk.Bbecause like
in some programs like ScreenFlow, you can be on camera at the same time where
you’re recording the screen, they can see you up in the corner of the screen and that
can be pretty cool. I don’t like the way I look with a headset on me. It looks like a geek.
If I’m doing some web cam video where I’m doing a web cam interview or something
then I’ll use a desktop mic. I don’t want to have anyone throw a headset out just
because I think it looks a little bit geeky. Some people do it and they pull it off but I just
don’t like the way they look on me.
!
If you are going to do a desktop mic, they are a little trickier to set up. They are
definitely impacted by the recording environment especially by the placement of the mic
and you can’t move around. You have to stay in the same place. That means generally
you’re seated. You don’t want to swivel around. You don’t want to turn your head. You
want to be talking directly into that mic. That’s very important.
!
A lot of times when I’m recording a screen cap video I want to be standing up because
I tend to have more energy when I’m standing up. Often I’ll even walk around a little
bit with my headset mic on. In fact right now I am standing up. I do think I bring more
energy with a headset mic because I can move around. The other thing is your chair
might be noisy. It might rock, it might swivel. When it does it might creek. When it does
that and you’re using a desktop mic it will pick up those sounds. It’s just something to
be aware of.
!
The cool thing about desktop mics is you don’t look geeky. In fact you can look uber
pro. It’s like there’s a shot of you and you’ve got a fancy mic that’s partially in the shot.
You can actually sort of look or at least some people can think it can look sort of cool.
!
Again, get a USB mic. Don’t bother with the XLR in my opinion. If you got a USB mic
you can plug in to any computer. I like the Audio-Technica AT2020. There are a bazillion
mics you can get out there. This one is pretty inexpensive. I can’t remember exactly
what it cost but I think it was about 100 bucks give or take and it’s a very high-quality
mic.
!
Okay. Next up we are going to go full motion video. I’m going to talk about full motion
video.
